title: myrsine lanceolata subsp. ouenarouensis
intro: Myrsine lanceolata subsp. ouenarouensis is a plant species, also known as Ouena or Ouéna, which is a subspecies of the Myrsine lanceolata tree, native to New Caledonia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ouena include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Myrsine lanceolata subsp. ouenarouensis typically involve the leaves and stems being dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
